plan.title: feat(ts-migration): cutover -- delete Python after full parity + settle installer question (Wave 4)

narratives.Description: feat(ts-migration): cutover -- delete Python after full parity + settle installer question (Wave 4)
narratives.Overview:
## Parent

#1530 (Part 1 of 2; umbrella epic #1545)

## What to build

Final cutover: once every gate/verb family has TS parity and a deprecation window has elapsed, remove `scripts/*.py`, `pyproject.toml`, and the Python CI lane; settle the Go-installer question (keep the Go single binary or fold into TS/Node). HITL gate -- requires explicit confirmation that all phases are confirmed working.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every Part-1 gate/verb family has passing TS parity and CI usage
- [ ] Python engine (`scripts/*.py`, `pyproject.toml`, Python CI) removed after the deprecation window
- [ ] Go-installer decision recorded (retain vs fold into TS/Node)
- [ ] `task check` passes on the TS-only engine

## Standing invariants (all Part-1 children)
- The Python suite is the **parity oracle** and runs **wholesale** (not affected-sliced) until deleted.
- `task check` stays green at every step (strangler-fig; no big-bang).
- The parity gate runs **cache-off** so a golden diff is never served from cache.
- No Python module is deleted until its TS replacement covers historical bug fixtures (not just happy-path) and CI usage.
- No agent-runtime dependency (that is Part 2, #1707); no monorepo orchestrator on day one (Nx never; Turborepo only on a measured trigger).

## Type

HITL
